question: Set 1 Winner: Kraus vs Akugue
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Sinja Kraus and Noma Noha Akugue in the Roland Garros, Qualification WTA, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Kraus” if Sinja Kraus wins the first set. It will resolve to “Akugue” if Noma Noha Akugue wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam match statistics.
groupItemTitle: Roland Garros, Qualification WTA: Sinja Kraus vs Noma Noha Akugue Set 1 Winner

events.title: Roland Garros, Qualification WTA: Sinja Kraus vs Noma Noha Akugue
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Sinja Kraus and Noma Noha Akugue in the Roland Garros, Qualification WTA, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Sinja Kraus' if Sinja Kraus advances against Noma Noha Akugue.

This market will resolve to 'Noma Noha Akugue' if Noma Noha Akugue advances against Sinja Kraus.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sinja Kraus held a clear edge in the Roland Garros qualifying matchup against Noma Noha Akugue on clay, driven by her higher WTA ranking near No. 92 and seeding as the No. 2 qualifier. Kraus arrived with strong recent clay form, including a three-set win over Celine Naef to reach the second round, while Akugue sat around No. 154-164 with limited recent success against top-tier opponents. Their head-to-head stood tied at 1-1 entering the match, though Kraus’s experience in Grand Slam qualifying and superior baseline consistency on the slower surface shaped trader consensus. The three-set battle ultimately highlighted Kraus’s ability to recover after dropping the opener, underscoring her edge in longer rallies typical of French Open clay.

question: Norrie vs. Navone: Set 1 Games O/U 10.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Cameron Norrie and Mariano Navone in the Geneva Open, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 11. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.
groupItemTitle: Geneva Open: Cameron Norrie vs Mariano Navone Set 1 O/U 10.5

events.title: Geneva Open: Cameron Norrie vs Mariano Navone
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Cameron Norrie and Mariano Navone in the Geneva Open, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Cameron Norrie' if Cameron Norrie advances against Mariano Navone.

This market will resolve to 'Mariano Navone' if Mariano Navone advances against Cameron Norrie.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cameron Norrie entered the 2026 Geneva Open clay-court event as the No. 3 seed and a semifinalist from the prior year, but recent form on the surface raised questions after a second-round exit at the Italian Open. Argentine Mariano Navone, a proven clay specialist ranked No. 42, carried strong momentum with multiple straight-set wins earlier in the tournament and held a 2-0 head-to-head edge, including a prior straight-sets victory over Norrie. Navone’s aggressive baseline game and backhand down-the-line consistency aligned well with the slow red clay, while Norrie’s results showed vulnerability against similar stylistic matchups. Traders weighed these surface and recency factors heavily in assessing implied probabilities for the Round of 32 contest.

question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 28°C on July 11?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent high pressure over the Anatolian plateau and clear skies are anchoring trader consensus around a 29–31°C peak for Ankara on July 11, with 30°C holding the highest implied probability at 34.5%. Official numerical models and climatological baselines place the July daily maximum near 30–31°C under typical ridge conditions, while recent forecasts from multiple sources converge on 29–31°C amid light northerly winds and negligible cloud cover or precipitation. The spread across adjacent bins reflects residual uncertainty in afternoon mixing and exact steering, as small shifts in model guidance or boundary-layer moisture could nudge the observed high by 1–2°C. Updated short-range runs from global ensembles will refine this range ahead of the July 10–11 period.

question: Exact Score: Independiente Santa Fe 2 - 1 Caracas FC?
description: In the upcoming Copa Sudamericana game between Independiente Santa Fe and Caracas FC, scheduled for July 23, 2026 at 8:30 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Independiente Santa Fe vs. Caracas FC match originally scheduled for July 23, 2026 at 8:30 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. All markets will settle based on the official final result as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. Revisions to officially declared final scores made after market resolution will not be accounted for in determining the outcome.

Recent draw results and continental scheduling point to a measured approach from both sides, with limited high-quality chances expected on a surface favoring control over open play. The tight clustering of implied probabilities across low-scoring outcomes reflects trader consensus on a cagey affair shaped by Santa Fe’s need to manage the aggregate while avoiding unnecessary risks, Caracas’s counter-attacking threats, and the absence of confirmed major injuries or lineup changes close to kickoff. Historical patterns in Sudamericana playoffs further support expectations of few goals and a competitive, low-event game.

question: Hammarby IF vs. Kalmar FF: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming Allsvenskan game between Hammarby IF and Kalmar FF, scheduled for July 12 at 8:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Hammarby IF and Kalmar FF combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on allsvenskan.se. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. eventMetadata.context_description: Hammarby IF, positioned in the upper half of the Allsvenskan table with a 6-2-4 record, hosts Kalmar FF on July 12 at 3Arena. Hammarby holds a clear edge in recent league form and home results, while Kalmar sits 12th with a 4-1-6 mark and winless away record. Limited confirmed injury updates include Hammarby's Koné (knee, out until September), with both sides otherwise expected to field near-full squads. Trader consensus on platforms like Polymarket aligns with Hammarby's stronger standing and historical dominance in this fixture, though Allsvenskan outcomes remain subject to typical variance in form and squad rotation.

question: Will Denis Bećirović be the Bosniak Presidency Member of Bosnia and Herzegovina following the 2026 general election?
description:
The Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina is a three-member collective head of state, consisting of one Bosniak member, one Croat member, and one Serb member. The Bosniak and Croat members are elected from the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, while the Serb member is elected from Republika Srpska. General elections in Bosnia and Herzegovina are scheduled to be held on October 4, 2026. 

This market will resolve according to the listed individual elected to serve as the Bosniak member of the Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina as a result of this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker Presidency members will not count.

If no such Presidency member has been elected by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Central Election Commission of Bosnia and Herzegovina (izbori.ba); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Bosniak President of Bosnia and Herzegovina?
events.description:
The Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina is a three-member collective head of state, consisting of one Bosniak member, one Croat member, and one Serb member. The Bosniak and Croat members are elected from the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, while the Serb member is elected from Republika Srpska. General elections in Bosnia and Herzegovina are scheduled to be held on October 4, 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: The October 2026 general election sets up a tight rematch for the Bosniak seat in Bosnia and Herzegovina’s tripartite Presidency between incumbent Denis Bećirović of the SDP-led “Troika” coalition and Bakir Izetbegović of the opposition SDA. Trader consensus reflects the rematch dynamics, with strong party bases among Bosniak voters in the Federation, ongoing fragmentation risks for the governing coalition over stalled EU reforms, and uncertain turnout patterns that could decide narrow margins. Semir Efendić’s minor candidacy adds limited vote-splitting potential without shifting the primary contest. Upcoming campaign developments, including coalition negotiations and polling trends in key cantons, remain the main variables that could widen the gap before voting on October 4.

question: Will the Cleveland Cavaliers make the 2027 NBA Play-In Tournament?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a spot in the 2027 NBA Play-In Tournament per the rules of the NBA. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to advance to the 2027 NBA Play-In Tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No". The Playoffs, as well as any tiebreaker, will not be considered part of the NBA Play-In Tournament.

If the 2027 NBA Play-In Tournament is cancelled, postponed to begin after May 15, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or the full 8-team Play-In Tournament field has not been officially confirmed by the NBA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA and/or the clinching team; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the 2025-26 NBA regular season, tight conference standings around the .500 mark shaped the play-in race, with Eastern teams such as the 76ers, Magic, Hornets, and Heat finishing between 43-39 and 45-37, while Western squads including the Suns, Trail Blazers, Clippers, and Warriors clustered from 37-45 to 45-37. Late-season form, home/away splits, and injury reports for key rotation players heavily influenced final positioning, as multiple clubs remained within a few games of the 7-10 seeds through April. Official injury designations and schedule difficulty in the final weeks determined which teams secured play-in berths ahead of the April 14-17 tournament, reflecting trader consensus on roster health and momentum rather than early-season projections. For the upcoming 2026-27 campaign, similar battles are expected among non-playoff clubs improving via free agency and draft additions.

question: Fabian Rieder: 1+ assists
description: In the upcoming FIFA World Cup game between Argentina and Switzerland, scheduled for July 11 at 9:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Fabian Rieder records more than 0.5 assists within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. This market will resolve to "No" if Fabian Rieder records 0.5 assists or fewer within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Assists are counted only if credited to Fabian Rieder in the official match statistics. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. If Fabian Rieder is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. events.title: Argentina vs. Switzerland - Player Props

eventMetadata.context_description: Argentina face Switzerland in the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarterfinal on July 11 in Kansas City, with Lionel Messi’s exceptional tournament form—eight goals and a record 21 career World Cup goals—driving heavy trader focus on his player props for goals, assists, and shots. Argentina advanced past Egypt 3-2 via a late comeback with no reported major injuries, fielding an experienced squad likely to control possession and create chances. Switzerland reached this stage by defeating Colombia on penalties but will be without key attacker Johan Manzambi, sidelined by a knee injury from training, which shifts emphasis to Granit Xhaka’s midfield influence and reduced Swiss attacking output in props. The matchup pits Argentina’s attacking momentum against Switzerland’s organized defense in a high-stakes knockout setting.

question: América FC vs. Londrina EC: Both Teams to Score in Second Half
description: In the upcoming Brazil Serie B game between América FC and Londrina EC, scheduled for July 13 at 6:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if both América FC and Londrina EC each score at least one goal during the second half of regular play plus second-half stoppage time. This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score in the second half (i.e., if one or both teams finish the second half with zero goals). If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to the outcome within the second half of regular play plus second-half stoppage time. First-half goals, extra time, and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. eventMetadata.context_description: América Mineiro hosts Londrina EC in Brazilian Série B on July 13 at Arena Independência, a matchup shaped by both sides' precarious league positions. América MG sits near the bottom of the table with just one win in its first 15 matches and a negative goal difference exceeding 15, though the home side typically benefits from strong crowd support and a deeper historical edge in head-to-head encounters. Londrina occupies 17th place, also in the relegation zone after mixed recent results that include defensive vulnerabilities on the road. Limited injury updates have surfaced for either squad, keeping focus on form, set-piece execution, and the need for points in a tight mid-table scrap. Weather or late roster tweaks could still influence the outcome in this evenly contested fixture.
